title: Parallel Wave Plan
description: Schema for generated wave plans with parallel task scheduling. Captures tasks grouped into execution waves based on dependency ordering, writeSet/sharedLocks for concurrency control, risk tiers, budgets, and expected produced facts. Governed parallelism: only independent fact changes can run concurrently; shared truth and high-risk boundaries remain serialized.

capturedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the wave plan was compiled.

waveId.description: Unique identifier for this wave plan (e.g. 'wave31-20260512').

waves.description: Ordered list of execution waves. Tasks within a wave may run in parallel; waves must execute sequentially (wave N must complete before wave N+1 starts).

conflictGroups.description: All conflict groups referenced by tasks in this plan. Tasks in the same conflict group must not run concurrently.

mainHealthSnapshot.description: Snapshot of main branch health at plan compilation time.

capturedAt.description: When the health state was last evaluated.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation for the health state.

wave.description: A single execution wave containing tasks that may run in parallel.

waveIndex.description: Zero-based wave ordinal. Wave 0 starts first.

riskTier.description: Highest risk level among tasks in this wave. Determines review requirements.

tasks.description: Tasks in this wave that may execute concurrently.

gatePolicy.description: How to handle task completion within this wave. all-complete = all must finish before next wave, any-fail-abort = any failure aborts the plan, best-effort = proceed regardless.

waveTask.description: A single task within a wave. Carries concurrency metadata, dependencies, and resource boundaries.

conflictGroup.description: Logical conflict group. Tasks in the same group must not run concurrently.

risk.description: Task risk level. High-risk tasks are serialized and require human review.

taskType.description: Task type from the worker contract.

workerClass.description: Task classification for routing and scheduling.

allowedFiles.description: Glob patterns the worker MAY edit.

writeSet.description: Exact file paths or narrow globs the worker is expected to write. Subset of allowedFiles. Used by the launcher to detect scope creep.

sharedLocks.description: File paths or globs that this task reads but does not write. Other tasks may also hold shared locks on these files concurrently.

validation.description: Shell commands the worker must run before opening a PR.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the fact asserts.

source.description: Where this fact was produced (issue number, PR, doc path).

dependsOnFacts.description: Facts that must be established before this task can run.

factId.description: Unique identifier for the fact this task will produce.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the fact will assert.

confidence.description: How certain the task is that it will produce this fact.

producesFacts.description: Facts this task commits to producing. Downstream tasks can declare these in their dependsOnFacts.

strategy.description: How to undo this task's changes if they cause problems post-merge.

blockedBy.description: Issue numbers that must merge before this task can start.

taskBudget.description: Resource limits for a single task.

budgetSummary.description: Aggregate budget reservations for the entire wave plan.

totalMaxFiles.description: Sum of maxFiles across all tasks in all waves.

totalMaxLinesChanged.description: Sum of maxLinesChanged across all tasks in all waves.

taskCount.description: Total number of tasks across all waves.

factGraph.description: Fact dependency graph for the entire wave plan. Tracks which facts are produced by which tasks and consumed by which tasks.

nodes.description: All facts referenced in the plan.

producerIssue.description: Issue number of the task that produces this fact, or null if pre-existing.

edges.description: Dependency edges: producer task -> fact -> consumer task.

producerIssue.description: Issue number of the task that produces this fact.

consumerIssue.description: Issue number of the task that consumes this fact.
